One more for history 👀

Up 3-0 on the Houston Rockets, a Game 4 win would mark the 13th sweep of LeBron’s career.

That would tie Kareem Abdul-Jabbar for the most all-time.
Another Rockies sweep.

The LeBron-led Los Angeles Lakers were 𝘁𝗵𝗶𝘀 𝗰𝗹𝗼𝘀𝗲 to their first sweep since the Kobe era.

May 2010. That was the last time the broom was officially out. We’re talking about Kobe Bryant at the peak of his powers, systematically dismantling a gritty Utah Jazz team.

Since that 4-0 demolition 16 years ago, we’ve seen championships, rebuilds, and the arrival of the King...but we haven't seen a sweep.

It is absolutely insane that we are even talking about a Lakers sweep in 2026. 

Let’s be real: the experts had the Lakers dead on arrival. The narratives were everywhere: "Too old," "The Houston Rockets’ youth will run them off the floor," "The window is closed." 

Then the real adversity hit. No Luka Doncic. No Austin Reaves. 🚑

Most teams would have folded. But instead, we’re watching a 41-year-old LeBron James defy Father Time yet again, making history with Bronny by his side.
